danger_signals.visual_form: Sprite-based hostile characters scattered throughout stages
danger_signals.meaning: Active combat threat; player must avoid or eliminate to progress

danger_signals.visual_form: Muted, low-saturation graphics dominance in level design
danger_signals.meaning: Postapocalyptic danger; environmental hazard communication through atmosphere

danger_signals.visual_form: Boss character sprite appearing in 'teaser' battles before final confrontation
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating threat; nemesis presence signals story progression toward climax

danger_signals.visual_form: UI element displayed bottom-left showing player vitality loss
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate danger state; visible countdown to player failure

safety_signals.meaning: Health recovery or ammunition replenishment; safe-to-approach resource nodes

safety_signals.visual_form: Transition from 1996 setting (Stage 1) to 2032 setting (subsequent stages)
safety_signals.meaning: Successful navigation; advance through world timeline indicates safety milestone

safety_signals.visual_form: Handgun, magnum, shotgun, and grenades available as pickups
safety_signals.meaning: Player empowerment; access to tools reduces vulnerability state

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Police-issue handgun; protagonist role as Sergeant John Spartan
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: 1996 urban setting (Stage 1) contrasts with 2032 postapocalyptic environment

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Simon Phoenix as visual antagonist; cryo-con henchmen in underground/cryo-prison levels
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Gunmen enemies; digitized sprites with varying AI behavior in later encounters
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Cryo-prison setting; underground level locations signify enemy strongholds

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stage 1 displayed in side-view platform setting; player enters via helicopter cable jump (movie scene recreation)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Initial mission structure; lower enemy density; establishes baseline before societal collapse

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: All stages after Stage 1 set in 2032; dreary, dark graphics palette; postapocalyptic setting visually conveyed through color desaturation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased enemy density; hostage rescue mechanics in overhead view stages; cryo-prison final location indicates dystopian control structures

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Final level features cryo-prison environment with Simon Phoenix as final boss
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must defeat Simon Phoenix 'for good' at location; climactic confrontation signals world-state resolution

environmental_storytelling.example: Stage 1 in 1996 establishes pre-collapse setting; subsequent stages in 2032 show postapocalyptic consequences without explicit exposition
Cryo-prison level location signals Simon Phoenix faction control; underground settings reinforce hidden enemy stronghold narrative
Colorful graphics in SNES version effectively distinguish 1996 urban stage from dark, dreary 2032 postapocalyptic environments

environmental_storytelling.technique: Hostage rescue as social collapse indicator
environmental_storytelling.example: Overhead-view stages require rescuing hostages to unlock doors; indicates civilization breakdown where rescue missions are core gameplay

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can fire in eight directions; movement speed normal; full access to weapons
Health bar diminishes; visual indicator prompts power-up seeking behavior
Scattered throughout stages; actively fire at player; require elimination or avoidance
Short-lived battles before final confrontation; signifies escalating threat progression
Cryo-prison encounter; defeat represents narrative climax and world-state resolution

notes: Demolition Man SNES communicates world-state primarily through environmental aesthetics and level structure rather than explicit UI text. The temporal progression (1996→2032) serves as primary narrative anchor. Danger is signaled through enemy density and visual atmosphere rather than explicit icons. The postapocalyptic setting uses color desaturation as key manifestation technique. Faction identity relies on location (cryo-prison for antagonists) and character sprite recognition rather than heraldic symbols.
